headline: SILVER CRASHES 3%+ TO $73 — OIL SHOCK AND IRAN WAR STOKE INFLATION FEARS BEFORE FED WEEK
snippet: Silver plunged over 3% on Tuesday to $73/oz — its lowest level since late March — as surging oil prices driven by the Strait of Hormuz blockade amplified inflation concerns heading into a packed central bank week featuring Fed, ECB, Bank of England, and Bank of Canada decisions.

headline: ARGENTA SILVER HITS 1,385 g/t OVER 4m — BRAND NEW HIGH-GRADE ZONE OPENS AT EL QUEVAR, ARGENTINA
snippet: Argenta Silver's fourth drill release from El Quevar returned 1,385 g/t Ag over 4.0m at the Mani-Copan target and identified a new high-grade zone 800m from the existing 45.3-million-ounce resource at 482 g/t Ag. The company is actively extending its resource footprint mid-campaign in Salta Province, Argentina.

headline: WORLD BANK SEES CEILING ON GOLD AND SILVER THROUGH 2026 — PRICES NEAR RECORD, UPSIDE CAPPED
snippet: The World Bank's latest commodity outlook warns that gold and silver face a price ceiling for the remainder of 2026, even as both metals trade near multi-decade highs. The bank cites persistent volatility, geopolitical tail risks, and stretched valuations as headwinds to further gains this year.

headline: ARYA RESOURCES NAMES "TRIPLET ZONE" AFTER HITTING 6.05 g/t AU OVER 10.85m IN SASKATCHEWAN
snippet: Arya Resources (TSXV: RBZ) declared a new gold discovery at Wedge Lake in Saskatchewan's La Ronge Gold Belt after step-out hole AR26-16 returned 6.05 g/t Au over 10.85m alongside unexpected broad sulphide-bearing iron formation at depth. CEO Rasool Mohammad called it a potential "major transformation" for the project.

headline: C3 METALS INTERSECTS 148m AT 0.42% COPPER IN PERU — 24 HOLES DRILLED AT KHALEESI PROJECT
snippet: C3 Metals (TSXV: CCCM) released 11 additional holes from its Khaleesi copper project in southern Peru, with standout intercepts of 148.05m at 0.42% CuEq from ~265m depth and 43.80m at 0.59% CuEq near surface. With roughly 11,000m drilled in 24 holes, the program is building a picture of a large-scale porphyry and skarn copper system.

headline: KODIAK COPPER OPENS 6,500m DRILL AT MPD PORPHYRY IN BC — 36 TARGETS, KETCHAN DEPOSIT FIRST
snippet: Kodiak Copper (TSXV: KDK) launched its fully-funded 6,500m 2026 drill program at the MPD copper-gold porphyry in southern British Columbia, opening the previously undrilled Ketchan deposit as the first target. The program builds on MPD's recently completed initial Mineral Resource Estimate, with 36 prospective targets identified on the property.

headline: USA RARE EARTH PAYS $2.8B FOR BRAZIL'S SERRA VERDE — TARGETS $550-650M EBITDA BY END OF 2027
snippet: USA Rare Earth agreed to acquire Serra Verde Group — owner of an operating rare earth mine and processing plant in Goiás, Brazil — for $300M cash plus roughly 127M shares, implying a $2.8B equity value. Serra Verde targets $550–650M in annualized EBITDA by end-2027, advancing U.S. efforts to build a non-China rare earth supply chain.

headline: U.S. MINT BUYING COLOMBIAN CARTEL GOLD FOR 20 YEARS — TREASURY SECRETARY ORDERS FULL PROBE
snippet: A New York Times investigation revealed the U.S. Mint purchased gold from suppliers tied to Colombia's Gulf Clan narco-cartel for over two decades without requiring provenance documentation. Treasury Secretary Bessent has ordered a full review of the Mint's gold procurement practices and supplier compliance with federal law.

headline: MONEY METALS: GOLD IS A RISK ASSET NOW, NOT A SAFE HAVEN — WAR ECONOMICS CHANGED THE RULES
snippet: Money Metals argues the Iran conflict has fundamentally repriced gold from a safe-haven asset to a war-driven risk play, with Western traders prioritizing Fed policy signals over geopolitical hedging. The piece challenges the conventional junior mining investor playbook on how to position in a protracted war economy.

headline: GOLD DROPS $125 IN ONE SESSION TO $4,577 — IRAN TALKS REVIVE AS CENTRAL BANK WEEK BEGINS
snippet: Gold fell $125 from the prior session to $4,577/oz on Tuesday as renewed US-Iran diplomatic talks reduced safe-haven demand, while investors adopted a cautious stance ahead of rate decisions from the Federal Reserve, ECB, Bank of England, and Bank of Canada. Gold remains $1,260 higher than one year ago.
